Here's all you need to know."]}],["$","p",null,{"children":[["$","strong",null,{"children":"TL;DR:"}]," ",["$","$Lb",null,{"prefetch":false,"href":"https://github.com/nrwl/nx/releases/tag/14.5.0","passHref":true,"replace":false,"scroll":true,"shallow":false,"children":"https://github.com/nrwl/nx/releases/tag/14.5.0","target":"_blank","rel":"noreferrer","className":"text-blue-600 transition-colors ease-out hover:text-blue-700 dark:text-sky-500 dark:hover:text-sky-400"}]]}],["$","h2",null,{"id":"cypress-v10-and-component-testing","className":"group scroll-mt-20","children":["Cypress v10 and Component Testing",["$","$Lb",null,{"aria-hidden":"true","href":"#cypress-v10-and-component-testing","children":["$","svg",null,{"xmlns":"http://www.w3.org/2000/svg","fill":"none","viewBox":"0 0 24 24","strokeWidth":1.5,"stroke":"currentColor","aria-hidden":"true","data-slot":"icon","aria-labelledby":"$undefined","className":"mb-1 ml-2 inline h-5 w-5 opacity-0 group-hover:opacity-100","children":[null,["$","path",null,{"strokeLinecap":"round","strokeLinejoin":"round","d":"M13.19 8.688a4.5 4.5 0 0 1 1.242 7.244l-4.5 4.5a4.5 4.5 0 0 1-6.364-6.364l1.757-1.757m13.35-.622 1.757-1.757a4.5 4.5 0 0 0-6.364-6.364l-4.5 4.5a4.5 4.5 0 0 0 1.242 7.244"}]]}]}]]}],["$","p",null,{"children":["Cypress v10 is probably the most significant update since Cypress was released. It comes with a new, exciting ",["$","$Lb",null,{"prefetch":false,"href":"https://docs.cypress.io/guides/core-concepts/cypress-app","passHref":true,"replace":false,"scroll":true,"shallow":false,"children":"Cypress App","target":"_blank","rel":"noreferrer","className":"text-blue-600 transition-colors ease-out hover:text-blue-700 dark:text-sky-500 dark:hover:text-sky-400"}],", component testing beta, a new JS/TS-based configuration file and much more. Read all the details in ",["$","$Lb",null,{"prefetch":false,"href":"https://www.cypress.io/blog/2022/06/01/cypress-10-release/","passHref":true,"replace":false,"scroll":true,"shallow":false,"children":"their official announcement","target":"_blank","rel":"noreferrer","className":"text-blue-600 transition-colors ease-out hover:text-blue-700 dark:text-sky-500 dark:hover:text-sky-400"}],"."]}],["$","p",null,{"children":"One of the strengths of Nx is to integrate various tools into a cohesive, high-quality experience. Working together with other companies and open source projects is key to making sure we meet this goal. We have had an ongoing relationship with the folks over at Cypress for years already and have been working closely with them since earlier this year to integrate v10 into Nx in the best possible way."}],["$","p",null,{"children":"This includes an upgrade script to automatically migrate Nx users using Cypress v9 seamlessly to v10. By running…"}],["$","$Ld",null,{"language":"shell","fileName":"","highlightLines":[],"lineGroups":{},"command":"","title":"","path":"","skipRescope":false,"enableCopy":true,"lineWrap":0,"children":"nx g @nrwl/cypress:migrate-to-cypress-10\n"}],["$","p",null,{"children":"…your workspace will be automatically upgraded to the latest Cypress version."}],["$","p",null,{"children":["Cypress v10 also comes with a beta version of ",["$","$Lb",null,{"prefetch":false,"href":"https://docs.cypress.io/guides/component-testing/writing-your-first-component-test","passHref":true,"replace":false,"scroll":true,"shallow":false,"children":"Component Testing","target":"_blank","rel":"noreferrer","className":"text-blue-600 transition-colors ease-out hover:text-blue-700 dark:text-sky-500 dark:hover:text-sky-400"}],". Nx v14.5 comes with an integrated generator to add component testing support to React-based project:"]}],["$","$Ld",null,{"language":"shell","fileName":"","highlightLines":[],"lineGroups":{},"command":"","title":"","path":"","skipRescope":false,"enableCopy":true,"lineWrap":0,"children":"nx g @nrwl/react:cypress-component-configuration --project=my-react-project --generate-tests\n"}],["$","p",null,{"children":["You can also append the ",["$","code",null,{"children":"--generate-tests"}]," to automatically generate Cypress component tests for the existing components in the target project (",["$","code",null,{"children":"my-react-project"}],")."]}],["$","$Ld",null,{"language":"shell","fileName":"","highlightLines":[],"lineGroups":{},"command":"","title":"","path":"","skipRescope":false,"enableCopy":true,"lineWrap":0,"children":"nx g @nrwl/react:cypress-component-configuration --project=my-react-project --generate-tests\n"}],["$","p",null,{"children":["Check out our ",["$","$Lb",null,{"prefetch":false,"href":"/nx-api/react/generators/cypress-component-configuration","passHref":true,"replace":false,"scroll":true,"shallow":false,"children":"generator docs","target":"$undefined","rel":"$undefined","className":"text-blue-600 transition-colors ease-out hover:text-blue-700 dark:text-sky-500 dark:hover:text-sky-400"}]," for more info."]}],["$","div",null,{"className":"text-center","children":[" ",["$","iframe",null,{"src":"https://www.youtube.com/embed/QDWN4C7T-Ck","title":"$undefined","width":"100%","allow":"accelerometer; autoplay; encrypted-media; gyroscope; picture-in-picture; fullscreen","loading":"lazy","credentialless":"true","className":"rounded-lg shadow-lg"}],"$undefined"]}],["$","h2",null,{"id":"globs-for-task-outputs","className":"group scroll-mt-20","children":["Globs for Task Outputs",["$","$Lb",null,{"aria-hidden":"true","href":"#globs-for-task-outputs","children":["$","svg",null,{"xmlns":"http://www.w3.org/2000/svg","fill":"none","viewBox":"0 0 24 24","strokeWidth":1.5,"stroke":"currentColor","aria-hidden":"true","data-slot":"icon","aria-labelledby":"$undefined","className":"mb-1 ml-2 inline h-5 w-5 opacity-0 group-hover:opacity-100","children":[null,["$","path",null,{"strokeLinecap":"round","strokeLinejoin":"round","d":"M13.19 8.688a4.5 4.5 0 0 1 1.242 7.244l-4.5 4.5a4.5 4.5 0 0 1-6.364-6.364l1.757-1.757m13.35-.622 1.757-1.757a4.5 4.5 0 0 0-6.364-6.364l-4.5 4.5a4.5 4.5 0 0 0 1.242 7.244"}]]}]}]]}],["$","p",null,{"children":["In v14.4 we ",["$","$Lb",null,{"prefetch":false,"href":"/blog/nx-14-4-inputs-optional-npm-scope-project-graph-cache-directory-and-more","passHref":true,"replace":false,"scroll":true,"shallow":false,"children":"introduced inputs and namedInputs","target":"$undefined","rel":"$undefined","className":"text-blue-600 transition-colors ease-out hover:text-blue-700 dark:text-sky-500 dark:hover:text-sky-400"}],". They allow you to fine-tune how caching works and when it should be invalidated."]}],["$","$Ld",null,{"language":"json","fileName":"nx.json","highlightLines":[],"lineGroups":{},"command":"","title":"","path":"","skipRescope":false,"enableCopy":true,"lineWrap":0,"children":"{\n    ...\n    \"targetDefaults\": {\n        \"build\": {\n            \"inputs\": [\"!{projectRoot}/**/*.spec.ts\"]\n        }\n    }\n}\n"}],["$","p",null,{"children":"Specifying such inputs can drastically increase the number of cache hits!"}],[["$","blockquote",null,{"className":"twitter-tweet border-0","data-conversation":"none","data-theme":"light","data-lang":"en","data-dnt":"true","children":["$","a",null,{"href":"https://twitter.com/victorsavkin/status/1550187124678205440","children":"Loading tweet..."}]}],["$","$L8",null,{"async":true,"src":"https://platform.twitter.com/widgets.js","charSet":"utf-8"}]],["$","p",null,{"children":["In this release, we also allow specifying globs for ",["$","code",null,{"children":"outputs"}],". Outputs are optional as Nx comes with reasonable defaults, but you can specify your own if your setup differs from the most commonly used ones:"]}],["$","$Ld",null,{"language":"json","fileName":"nx.json","highlightLines":[],"lineGroups":{},"command":"","title":"","path":"","skipRescope":false,"enableCopy":true,"lineWrap":0,"children":"{\n    ...\n    \"targetDefaults\": {\n        \"build\": {\n            ...\n            \"outputs\": [\"dist/libs/mylib\"]\n        }\n    }\n}\n"}],["$","p",null,{"children":["Globs are particularly useful when multiple targets write to the same directory. Say you have a ",["$","code",null,{"children":"build-js"}]," and ",["$","code",null,{"children":"build-css"}]," command and both write into ",["$","code",null,{"children":"dist/libs/mylib"}],". For reasons of clarity, if possible, our recommendation is to split them up. Like:"]}],["$","$Ld",null,{"language":"json","fileName":"nx.json","highlightLines":[],"lineGroups":{},"command":"","title":"","path":"","skipRescope":false,"enableCopy":true,"lineWrap":0,"children":"{\n  \"build-js\": {\n    \"outputs\": [\"dist/libs/mylib/js\"]\n  },\n  \"build-css\": {\n    \"outputs\": [\"dist/libs/mylib/css\"]\n  }\n}\n"}],["$","p",null,{"children":"Sometimes that's not feasible though. In that case, globs come in handy:"}],["$","$Ld",null,{"language":"json","fileName":"nx.json","highlightLines":[],"lineGroups":{},"command":"","title":"","path":"","skipRescope":false,"enableCopy":true,"lineWrap":0,"children":"{\n  \"build-js\": {\n    \"outputs\": [\"dist/libs/mylib/**/*.js\"]\n  },\n  \"build-css\": {\n    \"outputs\": [\"dist/libs/mylib/**/*.css\"]\n  }\n}\n"}],["$","p",null,{"children":["$","$Lb",null,{"prefetch":false,"href":"/reference/project-configuration","passHref":true,"replace":false,"scroll":true,"shallow":false,"children":"Read more in our docs","target":"$undefined","rel":"$undefined","className":"text-blue-600 transition-colors ease-out hover:text-blue-700 dark:text-sky-500 dark:hover:text-sky-400"}]}],["$","h2",null,{"id":"parameter-forwarding-when-building-dependent-projects","className":"group scroll-mt-20","children":["Parameter Forwarding when building dependent projects",["$","$Lb",null,{"aria-hidden":"true","href":"#parameter-forwarding-when-building-dependent-projects","children":["$","svg",null,{"xmlns":"http://www.w3.org/2000/svg","fill":"none","viewBox":"0 0 24 24","strokeWidth":1.5,"stroke":"currentColor","aria-hidden":"true","data-slot":"icon","aria-labelledby":"$undefined","className":"mb-1 ml-2 inline h-5 w-5 opacity-0 group-hover:opacity-100","children":[null,["$","path",null,{"strokeLinecap":"round","strokeLinejoin":"round","d":"M13.19 8.688a4.5 4.5 0 0 1 1.242 7.244l-4.5 4.5a4.5 4.5 0 0 1-6.364-6.364l1.757-1.757m13.35-.622 1.757-1.757a4.5 4.5 0 0 0-6.364-6.364l-4.5 4.5a4.5 4.5 0 0 0 1.242 7.244"}]]}]}]]}],["$","p",null,{"children":["Besides the speed aspect, one key feature of Nx is the ability to build dependent projects automatically. Let's say you have ",["$","code",null,{"children":"project-a"}]," which depends on ",["$","code",null,{"children":"project-b"}],", then whenever you run the build for ",["$","code",null,{"children":"project-a"}],", thanks to its project graph, Nx will automatically run the build for ",["$","code",null,{"children":"project-b"}]," first. You can define such dependencies either directly in your ",["$","$Lb",null,{"prefetch":false,"href":"/reference/project-configuration","passHref":true,"replace":false,"scroll":true,"shallow":false,"children":"project.json","target":"$undefined","rel":"$undefined","className":"text-blue-600 transition-colors ease-out hover:text-blue-700 dark:text-sky-500 dark:hover:text-sky-400"}]," or ",["$","$Lb",null,{"prefetch":false,"href":"/reference/project-configuration","passHref":true,"replace":false,"scroll":true,"shallow":false,"children":"package.json","target":"$undefined","rel":"$undefined","className":"text-blue-600 transition-colors ease-out hover:text-blue-700 dark:text-sky-500 dark:hover:text-sky-400"}]," file, or globally for an entire workspace in ",["$","code",null,{"children":"nx.json"}],":"]}],["$","$Ld",null,{"language":"json","fileName":"nx.json","highlightLines":[],"lineGroups":{},"command":"","title":"","path":"","skipRescope":false,"enableCopy":true,"lineWrap":0,"children":"{\n  \"targetDefaults\": {\n    \"build\": {\n      \"dependsOn\": [\"^build\"]\n    }\n  }\n}\n"}],["$","p",null,{"children":["The ",["$","code",null,{"children":"^"}]," is a short-hand notation for"]}],["$","$Ld",null,{"language":"json","fileName":"nx.json","highlightLines":[],"lineGroups":{},"command":"","title":"","path":"","skipRescope":false,"enableCopy":true,"lineWrap":0,"children":"{\n  \"targetDefaults\": {\n    \"build\": {\n      \"dependsOn\": [{ \"projects\": \"dependencies\", \"target\": \"build\" }]\n    }\n  }\n}\n"}],["$","p",null,{"children":["...and defines that the ",["$","code",null,{"children":"build"}]," task should be run for all its dependencies first."]}],["$","blockquote",null,{"children":["$","p",null,{"children":[["$","em",null,{"children":"You have a PNPM,NPM or Yarn workspace? Adding Nx doesn't only benefit you in terms of speed improvements, but also to define such build dependencies. Have a look at this video to learn more:"}]," ",["$","$Lb",null,{"prefetch":false,"href":"https://youtu.be/ngdoUQBvAjo?t=1485","passHref":true,"replace":false,"scroll":true,"shallow":false,"children":["$","em",null,{"children":"Setup a monorepo with PNPM workspaces and add Nx for speed: Defining task dependencies aka build pipelines"}],"target":"_blank","rel":"noreferrer","className":"text-blue-600 transition-colors ease-out hover:text-blue-700 dark:text-sky-500 dark:hover:text-sky-400"}]]}]}],["$","p",null,{"children":"What happens to parameters when invoking the target on a project's dependencies? By default, they are not forwarded but starting with 14.5 you can. Here are some configuration options:"}],["$","$Ld",null,{"language":"json","fileName":"","highlightLines":[],"lineGroups":{},"command":"","title":"","path":"","skipRescope":false,"enableCopy":true,"lineWrap":0,"children":"\"build\": {\n   // forward params passed to this target to the dependency targets\n  \"dependsOn\": [\n      { \"projects\": \"dependencies\", \"target\": \"build\", \"params\": \"forward\" }\n  ]\n},\n\"test\": {\n  // ignore params passed to this target, won't be forwarded to the dependency targets\n  \"dependsOn\": [\n      { \"projects\": \"self\", \"target\": \"build\", \"params\": \"ignore\" }\n  ]\n}\n\"lint\": {\n  // ignore params passed to this target, won't be forwarded to the dependency targets\n  \"dependsOn\": [\n      { \"projects\": \"self\", \"target\": \"build\" }\n  ]\n}\n"}],["$","p",null,{"children":["$","$Lb",null,{"prefetch":false,"href":"/reference/project-configuration","passHref":true,"replace":false,"scroll":true,"shallow":false,"children":"Read more in our docs","target":"$undefined","rel":"$undefined","className":"text-blue-600 transition-colors ease-out hover:text-blue-700 dark:text-sky-500 dark:hover:text-sky-400"}]}],["$","h2",null,{"id":"linting-performance","className":"group scroll-mt-20","children":["Linting Performance",["$","$Lb",null,{"aria-hidden":"true","href":"#linting-performance","children":["$","svg",null,{"xmlns":"http://www.w3.org/2000/svg","fill":"none","viewBox":"0 0 24 24","strokeWidth":1.5,"stroke":"currentColor","aria-hidden":"true","data-slot":"icon","aria-labelledby":"$undefined","className":"mb-1 ml-2 inline h-5 w-5 opacity-0 group-hover:opacity-100","children":[null,["$","path",null,{"strokeLinecap":"round","strokeLinejoin":"round","d":"M13.19 8.688a4.5 4.5 0 0 1 1.242 7.244l-4.5 4.5a4.5 4.5 0 0 1-6.364-6.364l1.757-1.757m13.35-.622 1.757-1.757a4.5 4.5 0 0 0-6.364-6.364l-4.5 4.5a4.5 4.5 0 0 0 1.242 7.244"}]]}]}]]}],["$","p",null,{"children":"We are obsessed with performance, yes we are! And I have good news: the Nx module boundary lint rule just got an order of magnitude faster 🤯."}],[["$","blockquote",null,{"className":"twitter-tweet border-0","data-conversation":"none","data-theme":"light","data-lang":"en","data-dnt":"true","children":["$","a",null,{"href":"https://twitter.com/meeroslav/status/1550058325236191232","children":"Loading tweet..."}]}],["$","$L8",null,{"async":true,"src":"https://platform.twitter.com/widgets.js","charSet":"utf-8"}]],["$","p",null,{"children":["Replacing ",["$","code",null,{"children":"Sets"}],", ",["$","code",null,{"children":"foreach"}],", ",["$","code",null,{"children":"reduce"}]," with plain ",["$","code",null,{"children":"for"}]," loops can often have quite a significant impact. You won't notice much on smaller projects, but on large Nx workspaces with 500+ projects you should see some huge improvements 🚀."]}],["$","h2",null,{"id":"support-for-banned-external-imports-lint-checks-on-transitive-dependencies","className":"group scroll-mt-20","children":["Support for banned external imports lint checks on transitive dependencies",["$","$Lb",null,{"aria-hidden":"true","href":"#support-for-banned-external-imports-lint-checks-on-transitive-dependencies","children":["$","svg",null,{"xmlns":"http://www.w3.org/2000/svg","fill":"none","viewBox":"0 0 24 24","strokeWidth":1.5,"stroke":"currentColor","aria-hidden":"true","data-slot":"icon","aria-labelledby":"$undefined","className":"mb-1 ml-2 inline h-5 w-5 opacity-0 group-hover:opacity-100","children":[null,["$","path",null,{"strokeLinecap":"round","strokeLinejoin":"round","d":"M13.19 8.688a4.5 4.5 0 0 1 1.242 7.244l-4.5 4.5a4.5 4.5 0 0 1-6.364-6.364l1.757-1.757m13.35-.622 1.757-1.757a4.5 4.5 0 0 0-6.364-6.364l-4.5 4.5a4.5 4.5 0 0 0 1.242 7.244"}]]}]}]]}],["$","p",null,{"children":["The ",["$","$Lb",null,{"prefetch":false,"href":"/features/enforce-module-boundaries","passHref":true,"replace":false,"scroll":true,"shallow":false,"children":"Nx Module Boundary lint rule","target":"$undefined","rel":"$undefined","className":"text-blue-600 transition-colors ease-out hover:text-blue-700 dark:text-sky-500 dark:hover:text-sky-400"}]," is a powerful concept especially when it comes to the maintainability aspect of projects and monorepos. Learn more in our blog article on ",["$","$Lb",null,{"prefetch":false,"href":"/blog/mastering-the-project-boundaries-in-nx","passHref":true,"replace":false,"scroll":true,"shallow":false,"children":"Taming Code Organization with Module Boundaries in Nx","target":"$undefined","rel":"$undefined","className":"text-blue-600 transition-colors ease-out hover:text-blue-700 dark:text-sky-500 dark:hover:text-sky-400"}],"."]}],["$","p",null,{"children":["The Module Boundary rule allows for much more though. It also allows to ban external imports. Say you have a frontend project where you want to make sure none of the \"backend-type\" dependencies accidentally get imported. Or vice-versa, a backend project where you wouldn't necessarily want to depend on any \"frontend-type\" package references. You can use the ",["$","code",null,{"children":"bannedExternalImports"}]," for that. For example:"]}],["$","$Ld",null,{"language":"json","fileName":".eslintrc.json","highlightLines":[],"lineGroups":{},"command":"","title":"","path":"","skipRescope":false,"enableCopy":true,"lineWrap":0,"children":"{\n  // ... more ESLint config here // nx-enforce-module-boundaries should already exist at the top-level of your config\n  \"nx-enforce-module-boundaries\": [\n    \"error\",\n    {\n      \"allow\": [],\n      // update depConstraints based on your tags\n      \"depConstraints\": [\n        // projects tagged with \"frontend\" can't import from \"@nestjs/common\"\n        {\n          \"sourceTag\": \"frontend\",\n          \"bannedExternalImports\": [\"@nestjs/common\"]\n        },\n        // projects tagged with \"backend\" can't import from \"@angular/core\"\n        {\n          \"sourceTag\": \"backend\",\n          \"bannedExternalImports\": [\"@angular/core\"]\n        }\n      ]\n    }\n  ] // ... more ESLint config here\n}\n"}],["$","p",null,{"children":["Note, the ",["$","code",null,{"children":"frontend"}]," and ",["$","code",null,{"children":"backend"}]," ",["$","code",null,{"children":"sourceTag"}]," definition is something you define. You could have easily named it differently. It is a string that can be attached to a project by adding it to the ",["$","code",null,{"children":"tag"}]," property of its ",["$","code",null,{"children":"project.json"}]," configuration file. Read more about banned external imports ",["$","$Lb",null,{"prefetch":false,"href":"/features/enforce-module-boundaries","passHref":true,"replace":false,"scroll":true,"shallow":false,"children":"in our docs","target":"$undefined","rel":"$undefined","className":"text-blue-600 transition-colors ease-out hover:text-blue-700 dark:text-sky-500 dark:hover:text-sky-400"}],"."]}],["$","p",null,{"children":["Starting with 14.5 we now support such checks also on transitive dependencies. Assume we have ",["$","code",null,{"children":"project-a"}]," and ",["$","code",null,{"children":"project-b"}],", both of which are tagged as ",["$","code",null,{"children":"framework-agnostic"}]," and have ",["$","code",null,{"children":"react"}]," in their banned external imports. Also, assume there's a relationship like ",["$","code",null,{"children":"project-a -> project-b"}],". If ",["$","code",null,{"children":"project-b"}]," imports ",["$","code",null,{"children":"react"}]," and we run linting, it fails correctly. However, if we run linting on ",["$","code",null,{"children":"project-a"}],", it succeeds as ",["$","code",null,{"children":"project-a"}]," is not importing ",["$","code",null,{"children":"react"}]," at all, thus not breaking the lint rule. In most situations, this is fine because linting happens at a project level, but sometimes you might want to have a \"transitive\" behavior where linting would also fail for ",["$","code",null,{"children":"project-a"}]," because it imports ",["$","code",null,{"children":"project-a"}]," which imports ",["$","code",null,{"children":"react"}],"."]}],["$","p",null,{"children":["You can now enable such behavior by setting ",["$","code",null,{"children":"checkNestedExternalImports"}]," to ",["$","code",null,{"children":"true"}],":"]}],["$","$Ld",null,{"language":"json","fileName":".eslintrc.json","highlightLines":[],"lineGroups":{},"command":"","title":"","path":"","skipRescope":false,"enableCopy":true,"lineWrap":0,"children":"{\n  // ... more ESLint config here // nx-enforce-module-boundaries should already exist at the top-level of your config\n  \"nx-enforce-module-boundaries\": [\n    \"error\",\n    {\n      \"allow\": [],\n      \"checkNestedExternalImports\": true,\n      // update depConstraints based on your tags\n      \"depConstraints\": [\n        // projects tagged with \"frontend\" can't import from \"@nestjs/common\"\n        {\n          \"sourceTag\": \"framework-agnostic\",\n          \"bannedExternalImports\": [\"react\"]\n        }\n      ]\n    }\n  ]\n  // ... more ESLint config
